questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Have you ever been to another country? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, I have. I've been to France and Spain.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Has she tried Thai food before? ___"
questions.answer: No, she hasn't. She wants to try it.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Have they seen the new museum? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, they have. They went last Sunday.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Has he ever won a competition? ___"
questions.answer: No, he hasn't. But he has been in two.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Have you and your sister met her new teacher? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, we have. She seems very nice.

questions.prompt: I _____ (visit) three different countries in my life.
questions.answer: have visited

questions.prompt: She _____ (not try) sushi before. It is her first time.
questions.answer: hasn't tried

questions.prompt: _____ you ever been to a live concert? I went to one last year.
questions.answer: Have

questions.prompt: My brother _____ (win) a football competition at school.
questions.answer: has won

questions.prompt: We _____ (not see) that film. Can we watch it tonight?
questions.answer: haven't seen

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I have went to London twice and I loved it."
questions.answer: 'Go' is irregular. The past participle is 'gone': 'I have gone to London twice.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She has visited Paris and she has saw the Eiffel Tower."
questions.answer: 'See' is irregular. The past participle is 'seen': 'she has seen the Eiffel Tower.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "My parents have travalled to many places around the world."
questions.answer: The correct spelling is 'travelled': 'My parents have travelled to many places.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We have watch that show three times and we still love it."
questions.answer: The past participle of 'watch' is 'watched': 'We have watched that show three times.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Tom has read the book and he have done all the exercises too."
questions.answer: 'He' takes 'has', not 'have': 'he has done all the exercises too.'
I have never ridden a horse.
Has she ever tried Japanese food?
We haven't seen that film.
He has visited two countries.
Have you been here before?
